title: Myasthenia Gravis Clinical Trial Pipeline Expands as 25+ Companies Race to Redefine Myasthenia Gravis Treatment Landscape | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: Over 25 pharmaceutical companies are advancing 30+ pipeline drugs for myasthenia gravis treatment, with approximately 5+ therapies in late-stage development. Key players including Immunovant, Cartesian Therapeutics, Novartis, and others are developing promising therapies using novel mechanisms of action such as IgG degraders and complement inhibitors. Recent developments include positive Phase III trial results and regulatory approvals for multiple candidates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company developing IMVT-1402 for myasthenia gravis with active pipeline advancement in a growing therapeutic space.
Developing Descartes-08 in Phase III trials with enrollment on track; therapy shows promise as an IgG degrader with potential late-stage approval pathway.
Advancing Iptacopan (complement factor B inhibitor) in Phase III trials, representing a major pharmaceutical player with significant resources in the myasthenia gravis space.
Cemdisiran (siRNA therapeutic) in Phase III NIMBLE trial showing positive data at 2026 AAN meeting with potential quarterly dosing advantage.
Zilucoplan (ZILBRYSQ) received positive CHMP opinion for pre-filled pen version in generalized myasthenia gravis, advancing toward European regulatory approval.
Claseprubart demonstrated positive Phase II results with rapid, statistically significant improvements and favorable tolerability profile in gMG trials.

title: Myasthenia Gravis Market Set to Surpass USD 16 Billion by 2036, Fueled by Rising Diagnosed Prevalence and Wave of Targeted Biologic Approvals | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The myasthenia gravis market is projected to grow from USD 6 billion in 2025 to over USD 16 billion by 2036, driven by rising diagnosed prevalence, improved diagnostics, and an expanding pipeline of targeted biologic therapies including FcRn inhibitors, complement inhibitors, and CAR-T cell therapies. The market is currently led by argenx and UCB, with numerous emerging candidates in late-stage development expected to significantly accelerate growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IMVT-1402 is highlighted as a strategically significant next-generation FcRn inhibitor with strong efficacy and favorable safety profile, with top-line results anticipated in 2027, positioning the company as a key player in the expanding MG market.

Descartes-08 is described as a potentially transformative CAR-T cell therapy with capability to address growing demand for safer, more durable treatment options, with Phase III enrollment on track as of November 2025.

Company has multiple candidates in development including FABHALTA (Iptacopan), a complement factor B inhibitor in late-stage development with Phase III data submission planned by 2027, and Remibrutinib, a selective BTK inhibitor with regulatory submission expected no earlier than 2028.

Gefurulimab, an anti-C5 albumin-binding bispecific antibody, has entered regulatory review phase with first major market submission completed, positioning it as a prospective new competitor in the gMG treatment landscape.

Currently a market leader in myasthenia gravis with robust product portfolio including VYVGART and VYVGART HYTRULO/VYVDURA, which is projected to generate the highest revenue among all therapies by 2036.

Market leader with strong product portfolio including RYSTIGGO and ZILBRYSQ; recent positive CHMP opinion in March 2026 for pre-filled pen version of zilucoplan further improves patient convenience and commercial footprint.
Company markets IMAAVY (nipocalimab-aahu), a marketed therapy representing the shift toward complement inhibition and FcRn blockade targeting core disease mechanisms.

Developing Pozelimab + Cemdisiran, a dual complement inhibitor combination in late-stage development, reflecting the evolving treatment paradigm toward targeting antibody production and B-cell activity.
Cladribine capsules (MAVENCLAD) is in the robust clinical trial pipeline for myasthenia gravis treatment, contributing to the expanding therapeutic options.

title: Crohn's Disease Eight-Market Drug Forecast and Market Analysis Report 2025-2026: Novel MOAs Will Provide Market Shifts Throughout the Forecast Period to 2032
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The Crohn's Disease therapeutics market across eight major markets is projected to grow from $9.5 billion to $13.8 billion by 2032 at a CAGR of 3.9%, driven by approvals of 8 pipeline, generic, and biosimilar therapies. Novel mechanisms of action and emerging treatments will reshape the competitive landscape, with key opportunities in strategic licensing and partnerships.

title: UCB - Oproeping tot de algemene vergadering van aandeelhouders 2026
author: Na
description: UCB NV announces its General Shareholders Meeting scheduled for April 30, 2026, covering routine matters including approval of 2025 financial statements, dividend distribution of €1.45 per share, renewal of board mandates for CEO Jean-Christophe Tellier and director Cédric van Rijckevorsel, and approval of control change clauses in the EMTN program. The extraordinary session will address renewal of board authorization for capital increases and share buyback programs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The notice presents routine corporate governance matters with standard shareholder meeting agenda items. The €1.45 dividend per share and renewal of board mandates indicate stable operations, but the document is procedural in nature without material business developments or strategic announcements that would warrant positive or negative sentiment.

title: UCB - Convocation à l'assemblée générale des actionnaires 2026
author: Na
description: UCB SA convenes its General Shareholders Meeting for April 30, 2026, to approve 2025 annual accounts, a dividend of €1.45 per share, and renew mandates for CEO Jean-Christophe Tellier and administrator Cédric van Rijckevorsel. The meeting will also address capital authorization renewal, share buyback authorization up to 10%, and approval of EMTN program change-of-control clauses.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The convocation presents routine corporate governance matters including dividend approval (€1.45 per share), board member renewals, and standard authorizations for capital management and share repurchases. No material negative or positive developments are indicated; these are standard annual shareholder meeting procedures.
